title: Rebuild Misato blues
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild Misato blues             Quote Post by Misato's toyboy » Sat May 17, 2008 12:51 pm I was one of those who bought the LE Japanese DVD, rather than downloading 1.01 digisubbed. I had hoped to get a charge from 1.01, but instead feel really disappointed. Its mainly because of how Misato is re-defined in the movie. Misato, the reason & the character that really made me an anime fan, has been changed into a much less likeable character. While still not as severe as (say) Emma Sheen in Zeta Gundam, Misato's mainly-friendly-big sister act has gone. What remains is a sympathetic soldier of vengeance, older, deeper-voiced, more authoritarian, more..stereotypically anime character. As I think of all the Misato moments I love in thr in the TV show, I'm wondering glumly what will be cut, or changed. And if I want to ever know or find out, let alone spend money doing so.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat May 17, 2008 12:57 pm Rebuild Subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat May 17, 2008 1:04 pm I do chuckle at her "My butt is cold" comment she makes.

[]:             Quote Post by Misato's toyboy » Sat May 17, 2008 3:11 pm Sachi_13 wrote: Rebuild Subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat May 17, 2008 3:13 pm Misato's toyboy wrote: I would have preferred Rebuild with new seiyuus, for its new look & sound.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat May 17, 2008 3:23 pm Misato's toyboy wrote: Sachi_13 wrote: Rebuild Subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat May 17, 2008 3:52 pm She's the same old condescending bitch in Rebuild that she was in TV and EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat May 17, 2008 5:24 pm BrikHaus wrote: She's the same old condescending bitch in Rebuild that she was in TV and EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat May 17, 2008 6:09 pm I certainly didn't see any difference in her character. Ok, they took out a little more of her fan service but I certainly didn't see any drastic changes. In fact the movie makes the relationship between Shinji and Misato more central as far as the first act is concerned. The only real BIG Moment missing between the two is when she finds him still waiting at the train track. They're relationship is the core one in the first film. And the Angels express it. When Sachiel attacks Misato has no real outlook on Shinji. He's just a tool to their cause. She could care less about him personally. By the second Angel she knows him as a person but she treats him as a mere subversive. Someone to follow her commands and when he breaks them she scolds him. But by Ramiel she's clearly come to see Shinji as an individual and someone to trust which is made crystal clear when she basically says to Gendo "Hey, shut the fuck up, your kids doing his best." In fact if I have any qualms with the first film its that they didn't change the characters enough. Though I'm surprised they didn't capitalize on Maya affections for Ritsuko. Lots of fan service potential there.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Sun May 18, 2008 3:48 am I mean, apart from fanservicy bits and the end of episode four, I see no dramatic change. I think I am slightly annoyed for character development purposes they left the end of four out, but it was good for Shinji to come out of his shell a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun May 18, 2008 10:25 am I find it hard to get a good feel for characterisation when it crams maybe 180minutes of story into 90. It reallty was too fast to catch every little detail, or every nuance on the first run through. Misato seems colder and harder, Shinji seems whinier and Gendou seems more...Gendou-ish. Characterisation seems to me to be a lot more polarised.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun May 18, 2008 10:33 am This is a quadrilogy guys, let's wait a little longer before we start complaining about a lack of characterization.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun May 18, 2008 10:49 am I'm only able to go on the first episode. I find the informatioin density is much to high for me to get a good feel of the characters. Theres too much going on and it's all too shiny.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun May 18, 2008 1:46 pm I think maybe she feels that way to you b/c there are less playful parts in the movie. It's really more due to restraints in time than anything I think.
